core_contribution: Stable-state and return-to-equilibrium assumptions dominate much ecological and systems thinking.
Resilience is distinguished from stability and linked to disturbance absorption before regime shift.
Alternative stable states slow variables thresholds and nonlinear change become central.
Management is framed as structured learning under uncertainty.
Growth conservation release and reorganization are theorized across nested scales.
Resilience expands into governance livelihoods institutions knowledge and transformability.
Resilience becomes central to ecological limits stewardship and transformation pathways.
Resilience enters policy language around hazards vulnerability preparedness and recovery.
Resilience is integrated with climate adaptation vulnerability development and long-horizon risk.
Scholars critique burden shifting and connect resilience to power justice and accountability.
